message:
Harden acfs-global / acfs-update / smoke_test.sh against PATH poisoning

The wrapper scripts and the embedded smoke-test library all relied on
PATH-resolved lookups for the small set of "trusted" helpers we use to
discover the installed acfs (id, whoami, getent, jq, sed, stat). On a
machine where PATH has been poisoned (an attacker-controlled binary
shadowing one of those names earlier in PATH), every call site became
a small TOCTOU/spoof surface: a fake `getent` could lie about the
target user's $HOME, a fake `id` could pretend to be a different
user, a fake `stat` could mislabel the owner of a directory, etc.

This change centralizes the lookup of those helpers through a single
helper that only accepts binaries living in /usr/bin, /bin (and for
the wrappers, /usr/sbin and /sbin). Every previously bare invocation
(id, whoami, getent, jq, sed, stat) now goes through that helper, so
even if PATH is hostile we resolve to the real system tool — or fail
closed if it isn't there. Three concrete shapes:

- scripts/acfs-global, scripts/acfs-update — extract a shared set of
  helpers (`system_binary_path`, `resolve_current_user`,
  `getent_passwd_entry`, `passwd_home_from_entry`, `read_state_string`)
  and rewire `resolve_home_for_user`, `resolve_current_home`,
  `read_target_user/_home/_bin_dir_from_state_file`,
  `read_user_for_home`, `find_acfs_bin*`, `find_installed_acfs_owner`
  to use them. acfs-update also stops using the unguarded
  `dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}"` idiom (which can blow up with
  set -euo pipefail when sourced from cwd) in favour of an explicit
  case/esac on the path shape.

- scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh — same hardening (`_smoke_*`-prefixed
  variants) for the in-repo and installed smoke entry points,
  including a /etc/passwd fallback when getent itself is missing,
  and a fall-through that no longer trusts a user-controlled
  ACFS_HOME without re-validating against the resolved system state.

- tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh — covers all three
  scripts: a new `test_smoke_repo_local_ignores_poisoned_explicit_acfs_home`
  exercises the smoke library, the rewritten `test_smoke_bootstrap_*`
  cases drop the previous `stat` mock (no longer load-bearing now that
  the script doesn't trust PATH `stat`), and a new
  `test_smoke_bootstrap_reads_state_with_poisoned_path` runs the whole
  flow under PATH=/nonexistent to prove the absolute-path fallbacks
  actually work.

@@ -25,6 +25,97 @@ sanitize_abs_nonroot_path() {
     printf '%s\n' "$path_value"
 }
 
+system_binary_path() {
+    local name="${1:-}"
+    local candidate=""
+
+    [[ -n "$name" ]] || return 1
+
+    for candidate in \
+        "/usr/bin/$name" \
+        "/bin/$name" \
+        "/usr/sbin/$name" \
+        "/sbin/$name"
+    do
+        [[ -x "$candidate" ]] || continue
+        printf '%s\n' "$candidate"
+        return 0
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+resolve_current_user() {
+    local current_user=""
+    local id_bin=""
+    local whoami_bin=""
+
+    id_bin="$(system_binary_path id 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$id_bin" ]]; then
+        current_user="$("$id_bin" -un 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$current_user" ]]; then
+        whoami_bin="$(system_binary_path whoami 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$whoami_bin" ]]; then
+            current_user="$("$whoami_bin" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    [[ -n "$current_user" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$current_user"
+}
+
+getent_passwd_entry() {
+    local user="${1:-}"
+    local getent_bin=""
+
+    getent_bin="$(system_binary_path getent 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$getent_bin" ]] || return 1
+
+    if [[ -n "$user" ]]; then
+        "$getent_bin" passwd "$user" 2>/dev/null
+    else
+        "$getent_bin" passwd 2>/dev/null
+    fi
+}
+
+passwd_home_from_entry() {
+    local passwd_entry="${1:-}"
+    local passwd_home=""
+
+    [[ -n "$passwd_entry" ]] || return 1
+    IFS=: read -r _ _ _ _ _ passwd_home _ <<< "$passwd_entry"
+    passwd_home="$(sanitize_abs_nonroot_path "$passwd_home" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    [[ -n "$passwd_home" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$passwd_home"
+}
+
+read_state_string() {
+    local state_file="$1"
+    local key="$2"
+    local value=""
+    local jq_bin=""
+    local sed_bin=""
+
+    [[ -f "$state_file" ]] || return 1
+
+    jq_bin="$(system_binary_path jq 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    if [[ -n "$jq_bin" ]]; then
+        value="$("$jq_bin" -r --arg key "$key" '.[$key] // empty' "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)"
+    else
+        sed_bin="$(system_binary_path sed 2>/dev/null || true)"
+        if [[ -n "$sed_bin" ]]; then
+            while IFS= read -r value; do
+                break
+            done < <("$sed_bin" -n "s/.*\"${key}\"[[:space:]]*:[[:space:]]*\"\\([^\"]*\\)\".*/\\1/p" "$state_file" 2>/dev/null || true)
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    [[ -n "$value" ]] && [[ "$value" != "null" ]] || return 1
+    printf '%s\n' "$value"
+}
+
 is_valid_username() {
     local username="$1"
     [[ "$username" =~ ^[a-z_][a-z0-9._-]*$ ]]
